classification.stance_proposed: research_blocked; do not advance to a security decision until the critical acceptance tests are met.

one_line_thesis: Delaware Basin produced-water infrastructure with strong Adj. EBITDA growth ($254M FY2025) and ~2,500 miles of pipe — asset-heavy midstream toll model, not Stahl croupier; thesis blocked by IPO/combination complexity and Five Point sponsor control.

deep_dive.executive_summary: WaterBridge is a scaled Delaware Basin produced-water network with filing-backed contract duration (~10.4-year weighted average remaining life) and a heavy 2026 build cycle. Component proofs value the existing network at about $36 per share enterprise value, less about $12 per share of net debt and a $1 per share execution reserve, plus a $3 per share contracted-growth option. At $34.75 we expect 6.49% per year on the total-synthesis base case over seven years. Moat remains unproven and dhando pending. We propose watch until cohort returns, contract quality, and refinancing headroom are rec...

human_review.model_stance: research_blocked; do not advance to a security decision until the critical acceptance tests are met.

pricing_analysis.conclusion: WaterBridge has valuable scale and contracted growth, but $34.46 is above the $26.51 base equity value and assumes successful execution of a $430M-$490M capital program with limited cost overruns. A base-case 15% entry price is materially lower; the current quote is a watch rather than an approval.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: The network may compound faster and with less risk than the model assumes because long-term dedications, MVCs, water intensity, and contiguous scale allow high-return brownfield expansion and rapid deleveraging after the 2026 build cycle.
pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Each Class A share corresponds economically to one OpCo unit. Valuation uses 47.016 million Class A plus 76.440 million Class B/OpCo units. The prior Class-A-only denominator materially overstated consolidated free cash flow per listed share.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: Each Class A share corresponds economically to one OpCo unit. Valuation uses 47.016 million Class A plus 76.440 million Class B/OpCo units. The prior Class-A-only denominator materially overstated consolidated free cash flow per listed share.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: The complete additive component schedule includes operating assets, financial assets, debt or financing claims, minority interests where applicable, and realization reserves before division by the stated economic units.

economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: The network includes approximately 2,500 miles of pipeline, 197 facilities, more than 4.5M bpd of capacity, and long-term dedications; tangible book value is not the primary valuation method.

component_groups.label: Existing produced-water network
component_groups.valuation_basis: $380M/$445M/$500M normalized EBITDA at 8x/10x/12x. A January 2025 Gravity Water transaction printed near 5.5x run-rate EBITDA, so WaterBridge's base requires a premium for scale, contracts, and network effects.
component_groups.overlap_control: The $10/$18/$28 per-share replacement-cost cross-check is embedded and never added to enterprise value.
component_groups.adjustments: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.

risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The component low/base/high range or milestone driver explicitly risks realization; uncertainty widens the range rather than creating a zero.
risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Discounting, realization friction, and the component assumptions reflect the time required to contract, develop, or monetize the claim.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining owner-funded capital is deducted in the driver where disclosed; otherwise the range and realization reserve reflect the unresolved capital burden.

component_groups.label: Net debt
component_groups.valuation_basis: March 2026 debt principal less cash across all 123.456M economic units.
component_groups.overlap_control: Deducted once from network enterprise value.
component_groups.adjustments: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.

valuation_proof.evidence: 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance is $425M-$465M. Values use $380M at 8x, $445M at 10x, and $500M at 12x.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.

valuation_proof.falsifier: Utilization, incremental after-tax return on capital, or contract economics fall below the low-case assumptions.

valuation_proof.evidence: Reserve for cost overruns, delayed in-service dates, and returns below the cost of capital on the $430M-$490M program.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Offsets only project execution risk, not recurring maintenance capital already reflected in normalized owner cash.
valuation_proof.falsifier: The obligation, financing claim, tax, or realization friction is materially larger than the low-case reserve.

valuation_proof.evidence: Pipelines, disposal capacity, rights-of-way, and permits provide an embedded asset floor but are already monetized by the core network.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: The $10/$18/$28 per-share replacement-cost cross-check is embedded and never added to enterprise value.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Utilization, incremental after-tax return on capital, or contract economics fall below the low-case assumptions.

wisdom_applied: Marathon/Capital Returns: a large build program creates value only when incremental returns exceed the cost of capital.
Marks/Oaktree: leverage and refinancing claims are deducted before attributing network optionality to common units.
Hohn: density, long-term contracts, and brownfield expansion can create a moat, but maintenance and growth capital must be separated.
Groundbreaker context: local water scarcity supports terminal relevance; it does not convert a capital-intensive operator into a capital-free royalty company.
limitations: Public cohort-level capex and EBITDA data are insufficient to verify management's incremental return claims.
The Gravity Water transaction is smaller and less contracted than WaterBridge, while public midstream peers have different commodity, basin, and contract mixes.
Replacement cost is not liquidation value and can exceed economic value when capacity is underutilized.

investment_committee.strongest_dissent: Reported EBITDA growth may be capital-purchased growth whose return falls below the cost of capital, while aggregate contract descriptions overstate cash-flow protection.
unresolved_items: Project-cohort invested capital, utilization and after-tax cash returns are not disclosed.

decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Discounting, realization friction, and the component assumptions reflect the time required to contract, develop, or monetize the claim.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: The component low/base/high range or milestone driver explicitly risks realization; uncertainty widens the range rather than creating a zero.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining owner-funded capital is deducted in the driver where disclosed; otherwise the range and realization reserve reflect the unresolved capital burden.

options.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Apply the production, contract, location, capital-intensity, ownership, timing, and realization adjustments described in _system/reviews/economic_value_comparable_audit_2026-07-15.md.
options.falsifier: The next decision milestone fails, remaining capital rises materially, or the stated success probability no longer fits observable progress.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.strongest_dissent: Reported EBITDA growth may be capital-purchased growth whose return falls below the cost of capital, while aggregate contract descriptions overstate cash-flow protection.
unresolved_items: Project-cohort invested capital, utilization and after-tax cash returns are not disclosed.
committee.next_action: Close the committee's primary-evidence blockers before requesting an owner decision.

gaps.question: What cash return has each major project cohort earned on total installed capital?
gaps.evidence_required: Project-level invested capital, in-service date, utilization, EBITDA, maintenance capital, taxes, and contract contribution for mature and ramping cohorts.
gaps.acceptance_test: Incremental ROIC is shown by cohort and reconciles to consolidated cash flow without counting contracted backlog as completed assets.
gaps.valuation_effect: Distinguishes genuine high-return reinvestment from EBITDA growth purchased with capital.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met: WBI's 2025 Form 10-K reconciles $254.0M adjusted EBITDA, $159.7M operating cash flow, $218.6M investing cash outflow, 1.622M bbl/day handling volume, and a 2,651-mile / 201-facility asset base (WBI/research/evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.md). It does not disclose project-level invested capital, in-service date, utilization, EBITDA, maintenance capital, taxes, or contract contribution, so cohort ROIC cannot be reproduced.

gaps.question: Project-cohort invested capital, utilization and after-tax cash returns are not disclosed.
gaps.evidence_required: Resolve the committee's missing fact with primary evidence and update the affected valuation proof row.
gaps.acceptance_test: The committee item is explicitly closed, sourced, and reflected in the valuation range or documented as immaterial.

gaps.question: How protected are price, volume, duration, and capital recovery under customer contracts?
gaps.evidence_required: Contract duration, minimum-volume or take-or-pay terms, escalators, acreage dedications, renewal rights, counterparty concentration, and termination provisions.
gaps.acceptance_test: Contracted and merchant cash flows are valued separately with explicit renewal and utilization assumptions.
gaps.valuation_effect: Sets the appropriate cash-flow duration, multiple, and downside case.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met: ~10.4y remaining life and acre dedication disclosed; contracted vs merchant split incomplete.

gaps.question: Can growth be funded through a stress case without diluting equity value?
gaps.evidence_required: Debt maturities, fixed/floating mix, covenant headroom, liquidity, committed capex, funding sources, and downside interest coverage.
gaps.acceptance_test: The downside case funds committed capital and refinances maturities while preserving explicit covenant headroom.
gaps.valuation_effect: Deducts financing cost and dilution risk from project value.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met: debt schedule and covenant thresholds disclosed; stressed sources-and-uses bridge incomplete.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: The network includes approximately 2,500 miles of pipeline, 197 facilities, more than 4.5M bpd of capacity, and long-term dedications; tangible book value is not the primary valuation method.

economic_claim.description: Each Class A share corresponds economically to one OpCo unit. Valuation uses 47.016 million Class A plus 76.440 million Class B/OpCo units. The prior Class-A-only denominator materially overstated consolidated free cash flow per listed share.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: The complete additive component schedule includes operating assets, financial assets, debt or financing claims, minority interests where applicable, and realization reserves before division by the stated economic units.

valuation_proof.overlap_control: The $10/$18/$28 per-share replacement-cost cross-check is embedded and never added to enterprise value.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Utilization, incremental after-tax return on capital, or contract economics fall below the low-case assumptions.

bullets.title: Insider · bearish
bullets.summary: WaterBridge Resources LLC reported an insider sale of 5,894,826 shares ($177,139,521).

timeline.label: Sponsor and Devon Energy sold ~17.5M+ shares in secondary offering near $30; large insider selling context.
Q1 2026 results: $102.9M adjusted EBITDA on ~2.5M bbl/d; 2026 EBITDA guide $425M–$465M and capex $430M–$490M.
Valerie Chase appointed independent director (term through 2026 annual meeting).
FY2025 Form 10-K: $254.0M adjusted EBITDA, $525.6M revenue, ~$1.46B debt; ~10.4-year weighted contract life.
IPO and combination with WaterBridge Resources LLC; Class A listing; sponsor Five Point retains significant control.
Insider purchases by Crane family and Gregory Daily at ~$20/share at IPO pricing.
Gravity Water acquisition referenced as comparable; smaller basin transaction near ~5.5× run-rate EBITDA.
Kraken and combination projects expanded Delaware network scale ahead of IPO.

industry.structure: Delaware Basin produced-water midstream is concentrated among a few scaled operators with pipeline density, disposal capacity, and long-term E&P dedications; merchant pricing, not regulated rate base.
industry.share_shift: WaterBridge gained share through IPO combination scale and brownfield expansion; Gravity and smaller private operators remain fragmented competitors.
industry.trend: Higher water intensity per well and basin consolidation support infrastructure demand; 2026 is a heavy growth-capex year (Speedway, Devon-supporting projects) with leverage and sponsor overhang as offsets.
